items.question: What's the difference between a logo and a brand identity?
items.answer: A logo is a single mark. A brand identity is the full system — logo, color palette, typography, tone of voice, and visual guidelines — that makes your business instantly recognizable across every touchpoint. We build the full system, not just the icon.

items.question: How do I know if my website is actually losing me clients?
items.answer: Common signs: high bounce rate, very few contact form submissions, visitors who don't scroll past the homepage, or feedback like 'I couldn't figure out what you do.' If your site doesn't clearly communicate your offer and make it easy to get in touch, it's costing you.

items.question: Do I need a new brand before investing in marketing?
items.answer: Not always — but weak branding makes marketing less effective. Ads and campaigns drive traffic to your site. If that site doesn't build trust or convert, you're burning budget. We often recommend fixing the foundation first so every marketing dollar works harder.

items.question: What marketing channels do you work with?
items.answer: We focus on channels that drive real leads: SEO (organic search), Google Ads (paid search), social media ads (Meta, LinkedIn), and email marketing. We'll recommend the right combination for your business — not a one-size-fits-all approach.

items.question: How quickly can you build a website?
items.answer: Most projects are complete in 3–6 weeks. Simple sites can move faster; more complex builds with custom features take longer. We set a clear timeline before we start and stick to it — no vague 'it depends.'

items.question: Can you help if I already have a website or brand but they're not working?
items.answer: Absolutely. Most of our clients come with something that exists but isn't performing. We assess what's salvageable and what needs to change, then build a clear plan — whether that's a light refresh or a full rebuild.

problem.heading: Most small business websites are costing you clients.

items.title: First impressions happen in 3 seconds.
items.body: If your site looks outdated, slow, or confusing, visitors leave before they even read what you do. You never get that moment back.

items.body: A website that doesn't convert is just an expensive brochure. You need clarity, trust signals, and a clear path to contact.

items.title: You shouldn't have to manage this yourself.

process.heading: Simple. Clear. No surprises.
process.subtext: Four steps from first conversation to a live, converting website.

steps.title: Discovery
steps.body: We start with a call to understand your business, your audience, and what you want your website to do. No jargon, no fluff.

steps.title: Design
steps.body: We create a custom mockup tailored to your brand. You see and approve the design before a single line of code is written.

steps.title: Build
steps.body: We develop your site with performance, SEO, and mobile experience in mind. You're kept in the loop throughout.

steps.title: Launch & Support
steps.body: We go live together. You get 30 days of post-launch support — questions, fixes, and adjustments, all covered.

faq.heading: Questions we get asked a lot.

items.q: How long does a website project usually take?
items.a: Most projects are completed in 3–6 weeks. Simple sites can move faster; projects with custom features take a little longer. We set a clear timeline from the start and stick to it.

items.q: Do I need to provide copy and images, or do you help with that?
items.a: You'll provide the core information about your business — you know it best. We guide you through exactly what's needed, help refine the messaging, and can connect you with photographers or copywriters if needed.

items.q: Can you redesign my existing website instead of starting from scratch?
items.a: Absolutely. Many clients come to us with a site that isn't performing. We assess what's working, what isn't, and either refresh the design or rebuild it properly — whichever serves your goals best.

items.q: Will my site work on mobile and load fast?
items.a: Always. Every site we build is mobile-first and performance-optimized. We test across devices and screen sizes before launch so you can be confident it works everywhere.

items.q: What happens after the website goes live?
items.a: Every project includes 30 days of post-launch support — fixes, minor adjustments, and guidance. After that, we offer ongoing maintenance or hand you off with full documentation.

items.q: How is this different from a builder like Squarespace or Wix?
items.a: Template builders give you a generic starting point. We build something specific to your business, your audience, and your goals — a site that converts better, loads faster, and looks like it belongs in the premium tier of your market.

form.description: No pitch. No pressure. We'll review your current website (or brief) and give you honest feedback on what's holding it back.
checklist: Free, no-strings review of your current site

form.noCommitment: No pitch. No commitment. We'll reply within 1 business day.

hero.h1_before: Find out exactly why your website is
hero.lede: Tell us about your site. We'll personally review it and send you honest feedback on what's working, what's not, and what to fix first — within 24 hours.

features.lede: We personally go through your website and send you honest, direct feedback on what's working, what's costing you, and what to fix first.

cards.title: A real person reviews your site
cards.body: Not an algorithm. Not a checklist tool. Someone who does this professionally goes through your website and tells you what they see.

cards.title: Built around your specific site
cards.body: The feedback is based on your actual website — not a generic template. What you get is relevant to your business, your audience, and your goals.

cards.title: No fluff in the feedback
cards.body: Every word earns its place. If it doesn't help you act, it's not in there.

cards.title: Action over insight
cards.body: The review exists to create motion. Clear on what to fix, and in what order — not a lecture on why things matter.

cards.title: Straight to your inbox
cards.body: We send your feedback within 24 hours. No spam, no drip sequences you didn't ask for.

cards.title: The same lens we use on paid projects
cards.body: This is the exact review process we run before taking on any new client. You get the same quality of thinking — free.

steps.lede: No quiz. No score. Just tell us about your site and we'll take it from there.

items.title: Fill in the form
items.body: Your name, email, website URL, and optionally what's frustrating you most about your current online presence. Takes about 2 minutes.

items.title: We review your site
items.body: We go through your website the way a potential customer would — looking at what makes them stay, leave, trust you, or walk away.

items.title: You get honest feedback within 24 hours
items.body: A direct, personal review of your specific site sent to your inbox. Not a generic checklist — feedback that actually applies to your business.

panes.heading_before: Stop losing leads to rivals with a
panes.body: Plumbers, lawyers, cleaners — your clients look you up before they call. We'll tell you exactly why they're choosing the competition, and what to fix first.
People search before they walk in. We check whether your online presence matches the quality of your store — and where it falls short.
Traffic without sales is a signal problem. We look at trust gaps, friction points, and conversion killers that are quietly costing you revenue.
Clients vet you before they email. We'll tell you whether your site passes the trust bar — or quietly loses you work.
Use the free audit as a conversation starter. Share a client's feedback before the pitch — it's the most compelling opener you'll ever send.

frustration.label: What's your biggest frustration with your current online presence?
frustration.placeholder: Optional — but the more you tell us, the more useful the feedback

form.success: We've received your site. You'll hear from us within 24 hours.
form.error: Something went wrong. Please try again or email us at hello@lumiq.studio
form.microcopy: We reply within 24 hours. No spam, no sales pressure.

items.a: Yes. No card, no hidden catch. We review your site and send you honest feedback. What you do with it is up to you.

items.q: What do you actually look at?
items.a: We look at your site the same way a potential customer would — your first impression, how easy it is to understand what you do, whether you build trust, and whether there's a clear path to contact you.

items.q: How long does the review take?
items.a: We aim to get your feedback to you within 24 hours of receiving your form.

items.q: What if I don't have a website yet?
items.a: Tell us that in the form. We can still give you useful direction based on your current online presence and what you're trying to achieve.

items.q: What do you do with my email?
items.a: We use it to send you your feedback and, if it's relevant, a short follow-up. You can ask us to stop any time.

items.q: Can I request a review for a client's website?
items.a: Absolutely. Many consultants use this as a prospecting tool — review a potential client's site and share the feedback to open the conversation.

finalCta.lede: Two minutes to fill in. Free. A real, personal look at what's holding your website back.
finalCta.microcopy: No card · No spam · We reply within 24 hours

Problem.lede: You've shipped the product. You've written the posts. You've shown up consistently for months. And yet the inbox stays quiet, the launches land soft, and the right people never seem to find you.
Problem.lede_em: It isn't the work — it's the signal.

cards.body: The right buyers exist. They're searching. They're scrolling. But your name never enters the room — and "trying harder" hasn't moved the needle in months.
What you actually do is good — sometimes great. But the way it's told online buries the lead. Visitors leave without ever understanding why you, and why now.
No replies. No saves. No DMs. No "where did you come from?" intros. Without measurable visibility, every next move is a guess — and guessing is expensive.
